revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:備後国風土記]][[zh:備後國風土記]]


'''Bingo no Kuni Fudoki''' (備後国風土記, also read as 吉備道後国風土記) is the [[Fudoki]] of {{ill|Bingo Province|en|Bingo Province|qid=Q1049113|lt=Bingo Province|1=Bingo Province}}, compiled during the early {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|qid=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}}.
In the mid-{{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|qid=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}}, the story of [[:en:Somin Shorai|Somin Shorai]] was preserved in the ''{{ill|Shaku Nihongi|en|Shaku Nihongi|qid=Q2710483|lt=Shaku Nihongi|1=Shaku Nihongi}}'' by [[Urabe Kanekata]] as an excerpt from the "Bingo no Kuni Fudoki."
== Excerpt from ''Shaku Nihongi'': "Bingo no Kuni Fudoki Fragment" ==
{{Verse translation
|head1=Original Text|lang1=ojp
|備後國風土記曰 疫隅國社 昔 北海坐志武塔神 南海神之女子乎與波比爾出 座爾日暮 彼所將來二人在伎 兄蘇民將來 甚貧窮 弟將來富饒 屋倉一百在 伎 爰武塔神 借宿處 惜而不借 兄蘇民將來借奉 即以粟柄爲座 以 粟飯等饗奉 爰畢出坐 後爾經年 率八柱子還來天詔久 我將來之爲報 答 汝子孫其家爾在哉止問給 蘇民將來答申久 己女子與斯婦侍止申 即詔久 以茅輪 令着於腰上 隨詔令着 即夜爾 蘇民之女子一人乎置天 皆悉 許呂志保呂保志天伎 即詔久 吾者 速須佐雄能神也 後世爾疫氣在者 汝蘇民 將來之子孫止云天 以茅輪着腰在人者 將免止詔伎（釋日本紀卷七）
|attr1=''Shaku Nihongi,'' Volume 7
|head2=Translation|lang2=en
|The ''Bingo no Kuni Fudoki'' states: The ''Ekusuma-no-kuni Shrine'' in ancient times enshrined the deity Mutafu of the northern sea, who went out to court the daughter of the deity of the southern sea. As dusk fell, Mutafu arrived at the place of two men named Shorai. The elder brother, Somin Shorai, was extremely poor, while the younger brother was wealthy and owned a hundred warehouses. Mutafu requested lodging, but the wealthy younger brother refused. However, Somin Shorai granted the request, making a seat of millet stalks and serving millet rice as hospitality. 
After some time passed, Mutafu returned with eight divine children and declared, "I will repay your kindness. Are your descendants still living in this house?" Somin Shorai replied, "Only my daughter and I remain." Mutafu then instructed them to fashion ''chino-wa'' (wreaths of cogon grass) and wear them around their waists. Following the instruction, they did so. That night, only Somin’s daughter survived, while all others were destroyed. Mutafu then revealed himself as {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, proclaiming, "In future generations, during times of epidemics, those who wear ''chino-wa'' and declare themselves descendants of Somin Shorai shall be spared."
}}
== Notes ==
{{reflist|qq=}}
== External Links ==
* ''[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1173165/161 Fudoki],'' p.315: "Bingo Province," edited by {{ill|Yūkichi Takeda|en|Yūkichi Takeda|qid=Q2165204|lt=Takeda Sukeyoshi|1=Yūkichi Takeda}}, Iwanami Shoten (National Diet Library).
